paragraphs: Review the assigned material before class and note any terms that require clarification during discussion.
Bring a copy of your notes and be prepared to compare examples with a partner during the opening activity.
Responses may be concise, but they should refer to evidence from the reading or the lecture summary.
Use complete sentences for short written answers unless the prompt explicitly requests a list or outline.
Office hours are intended for content questions, assignment planning, and feedback on study strategies.
The goal of this handout is to support organized review rather than to introduce new material.
questions: Identify one claim made by the author and explain why it matters for the broader topic.
Summarize the most relevant example from the reading in two or three sentences.
List two terms from the lecture that connect directly to the assigned material.
Describe one question you would raise during class discussion and explain what prompted it.
Compare the main topic this week with a topic covered earlier in the course.
Write a short response explaining which concept still feels least familiar.
